## The Project: Satellite Imagery at Insee

- Goal: [**detect buildings on satellite imagery**]{.orange} across French overseas territories (OTs)
- Identify areas with the [**strongest changes in built-up land**]{.blue2} — constructions & demolitions
- Priority targets: [**informal settlements**]{.blue2} in French OTs
- Used to [**assist and optimise cartographic surveys**]{.orange} conducted before each census

::: {.notes}
The goal of this project is to **detect buildings in satellite imagery** in order to identify areas experiencing the most significant changes in the built environment—whether through new construction or demolition. The objective is to monitor areas with high levels of "activity," such as **shantytowns in the French Overseas Departments and Regions (DROM)**.
:::

## Final product and pipeline

*Demo*: [web application](https://inseefrlab.github.io/satellite-images-webapp/)

*Image: pipeline of the project*
![](../img/pipeline_donnees_sat_en.png)

## Full technical stack of the project

Entire pipeline built and run on [**Onyxia**]{.orange}
![](../img/app-architecture.png){width=60%}

## Use Case: Cyclone Chido

- Cyclone Chido hit [**Mayotte in late 2024**]{.orange} — one of the most devastating in decades
- Using our pipeline, we produced a [**full post-cyclone land cover map**]{.blue2} and identified destructions in [**a single night of computation**]{.orange}

::: {.notes}
This project was **developed entirely on Onyxia**, and its pipeline consists exclusively of Onyxia components. During **Cyclone Chido in Mayotte in late 2024**, it was possible to generate a post-cyclone land-cover map and identify areas of destruction in **a single night of processing**. We will walk through this pipeline step by step to explain it in detail.

> Note: The project's backend is developed entirely in **Python**.
:::
